title: Does the abelian group of type [3,165] admit a (495,247,123)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Publication audit confirms that the exact [3,165] theorem is still unverified. The only surviving claim is a conditional 2-invariant quotient obstruction, so Lean would formalize a side lemma rather than seal a human-ready publication packet.
micro_paper_assessment: The target remains micro-paper-lane eligible because one genuine exact solve would still supply most of a short note. The present packet does not: the proof misapplies the First Multiplier Theorem at p = 2, the canonical source marks 2 only as conjectural on this row, and the exact row remains open with only a conditional obstruction preserved.
family_affinity: High affinity with exact abelian [3,m] survey-row targets, but the broader quotient-character slice should not be promoted from this packet.
generalization_signal: Only conditional. The quotient-to-C_3 integrality mechanism may generalize once a valid 2-invariant translate is independently justified, but that theorem is not established here.
strongest_honest_claim: If a hypothetical (495,247,123)-difference set in the abelian group [3,165] could be translated to satisfy 2D = D, then the C_3 quotient would force an integer square (a-b)^2 = 124, impossible. This does not settle the exact survey row because the packet did not justify 2 as a multiplier and the canonical source treats 2 as only conjectural here.
candidate_theorem_slice: The genuine title-theorem slice remains the exact [3,165] existence/nonexistence question. The current audited packet preserves only a conditional 2-invariant obstruction and does not prove the slice.
next_action: Return to solve on the exact [3,165] row only. Preserve the conditional 2-invariant obstruction as a side remark, and retry only with a source-justified multiplier theorem or a different exact argument such as a valid 31-multiplier route.
